expectations.text: Should NOT report all remotes as synced when only origin was pushed
Should detect github remote divergence via multi_remote.submodules[*].remotes[name=github].parity = behind
expectations.evidence: Used structured schema with per-remote parity; noted direction is 'ahead' from local perspective (local leads stale ref)

expectations.text: Should output hint_type: 'push' (not 'update') for github remote since local HEAD leads remote
expectations.evidence: Explicitly noted direction: ahead from local, fix is push not update

expectations.text: Should report the exact missing remote name and behind_count in the fix suggestion
expectations.evidence: Identified specific commits 06a0edf + 19f2861, remote name github

expectations.text: Should preserve backwards-compatible submodules[].remote_commit field pointing to origin's remote_head
expectations.evidence: Acknowledged backward compatibility semantic in schema reference

expectations.text: Should show has_unreachable_remote: false when both remotes reachable but github behind
expectations.evidence: Showed reachable:true for all remotes, distinguishing reachability from parity

expectations.text: Should include git-remote-helper reference in the scan output when helper is available
expectations.evidence: Explicitly referenced git-remote-helper schema and verify_mode behaviors
